sutta: 1 morning the venerable ananda robing himself early took bowl and rope and went to the house of the lay disciple migasala and there sat down on a seat mate ready and meegasala came and saluted him and sat down at 1 side so seated she said to the venerable ananda pray raven sir how ought 1 to understand this dharma taught by the exalted 1 that both he who lives the holy life and he who does not shall become light wayfarers in the world to come my father sir purana lived the holy life dwelling apart abstaining from common carnal things and when he died the exalted 1 explained he is a once returner dwelling into sita heaven my uncle sir isidata did not live the holy life but rejoiced with a wife and of him also when dead the exalted 1 said he is a once returner dwelling into sita heaven reverend ananda how ought 1 to understand this dhamma and when bernanda said even sister as the exalted 1 has said and when the vulnerable ananda had received arms in me gasala's house he rose from his seat and departed now stop here for a while just to commend that probably ananda member ananda could not explain to this amiga salah that's why he said you just remember it as the exalted 1 said and now the suta continues now on his return from arms gathering after his meal the verbal ananda visited the exalted 1 saluted him and sat down at 1 side and so seated he told the exalted 1 all that had occurred adding i told her lord to understand the matter even as the exalted 1 had had explained and the buddha said but ananda who is the lay disciple of mikasala a foolish frail motherly body with but mother with to understand the diversity in the person of man these 6 persons ananda are found in the world what 6 consider ananda 1 well restrained a comely person in whose company his fellows in the holy life take pleasure yet in whom hearing dhamma is of no effect much learning is of no effect in whom there is no view penetration who wins not partial release he on the breaking up of the body after death sets out to fall not to excel fears to afford fears not to excellence consider another well restrained likewise but in whom hearing dhamma has effect much learning has effect in whom there is view penetration who wins partial release he after death sets out to excel not to fall fares to excellence not to a fall and the measurers measure them saying his stature is just this the others just that in what way is 1 wanting 1 exalted and that measuring ananda is to the measurers harm and hurt for many a day now the 1 in whom listening has effect who wins partial release that person ananda has marched further forward is more exalted than the former and why is that the stream of dhamma carries him forward ananda but who saved the tatar gather can judge that difference therefore ananda be no measurer of persons measure not the measure of persons fairly ananda he digs a bit for himself who measures the measure of persons i alone ananda can measure their measure or 1 like me consider ananda a person in whom wrath and pride are conquered but in whom greed from time to time surges in whom hearing dhamma is of no effect and another in whom hearing has effect he after death sets out to excel not to fall fears to excellence not to afford that person has much further forward is more exalted than the former i alone ananda can measure their measure consider 1 in whom wrath and pride are conquered but in whom the world of words from time to time surges in whom hearing dhamma and learning are of no effect in whom there is no view penetration who wins not partial release he after death sets out to fall not to excel fears to afford fares not to excellence and another likewise but in whom hearing dhamma and learning have effect in whom there is view penetration who wins partial release he after death sets out to excel not to fall fast to excellence not to a fall and the measurers measure them likewise and it is through their harm and hurt for many a day and in whom hearing dharma and learning have effect that 1 has much further forward is more exalted than the former and why the dhamma stream carries him forward but who saved the tatar gather can judge this difference therefore ananda be be no measurer of persons measure not the measure of persons barely ananda he digs a pit for himself who measures the measure of persons i alone ananda can measure their measure or 1 like me and who is the lay disciple me gasala a foolish frail motherly body with but mother with to understand the diversity in the person of man fairly ananda these 6 persons are found in the world ananda with such virtue as purana was endowed isidata may become endowed hearing purana fears not isidata's way but another's with such insight as isidata was endowed purana may become a doubt hearing easy data fears not purana's way but another's thus verily ananda both these men are wanting in 1 thing that's the end of the suta
commentary:
here this lady meega salah she was a bit perplexed because the father and the uncle both when they died the buddha said that they were both once returners that means sakata garmin second stage uh fruition person but 1 of them was a layperson the other 1 was a monk so this might allah couldn't understand why 1 is a lay person 1 is a monk but yet both of them obtain the same same fruit of the holy life so the buddha said explain the 6 types of persons found in the world and the buddha said not to measure another person in the buddha be measured be no measure of persons measure not the measure of persons very ananda he digs a pit for himself who measures the measure of persons so here the buddha is giving a warning that it is dangerous to judge other people especially a person who has the rope it's very difficult for lay people to judge so we have to remember to be very careful then when we criticize some monks because sometimes you might think a monk has a wrong view and he may have right view especially if among practices and keeps the precepts and meditates and you have to be very very careful and not to criticize such a manga or develop hatred for such a manga uh in the end the buddha said now that purana was although both of them purana and ec data were once returners but purana excelled in virtue whereas easy data excelled in inside so so both of them they had slightly different qualities but both of them are sakataka means

items: one in whom wrath and pride are conquered
another in whom wrath and pride are conquered
